summary: Ported the old object-file library list from `obj_files.mk` into `configure.py` by adding a large `config.libs` inventory of `MeleeLib(...)` groups and `Object(Matching/NonMatching, ...)` entries. The PR explicitly noted that it did not add splits or symbols yet, so it mostly established configuration data rather than changing the ninja build behavior immediately.

key_files.role: Only changed file; gained the ported object/library configuration previously represented by `obj_files.mk`, including `MeleeLib`, `RuntimeLib`, `Matching`, `NonMatching`, and many `Object(...)` entries.

what_changed: Added a `RuntimeLib(lib_name: str, objects: Objects) -> LibDict` helper that currently delegates directly to `Lib(lib_name, objects)`.

Expanded `config.libs` with a large list of `MeleeLib(...)` library groups, starting with `lb (Library)`, `cm (Camera)`, `pl (Player)`, `mp (Map)`, `ef (Visual effects)`, and `ft (Fighters)` in the excerpt.
Represented each source file as `Object(Matching, "path.c")` or `Object(NonMatching, "path.c")`, preserving whether a translation unit was matching or still nonmatching.

Included cases where the same source appears both as `Matching` and `NonMatching`, such as `melee/lb/lbcollision.c`, `melee/lb/lbarchive.c`, `melee/ft/ftcoll.c`, and several fighter common-state files, reflecting split or partial-match realities rather than forcing a single status.
Kept library grouping and in-list comments to preserve source ordering/context, including sections like `# Main`, `# Common`, `# Main again`, and fighter-specific subsections.
The PR body states this did not add splits or symbols and therefore did basically nothing to the `ninja` build process yet.
The PR body also calls out orphaned data files, for example `baselib_shared_data_001`, as products of erroneous splits to iron out later.
smart_moves: Moved object inventory into Python configuration, making the build metadata part of `configure.py` instead of leaving it in a separate makefile-style list.
Preserved `Matching` and `NonMatching` as explicit booleans/aliases, keeping each object entry compact while making status searchable and easy to update.

Grouped objects by Melee subsystem/library labels such as `lb (Library)`, `cm (Camera)`, `pl (Player)`, `mp (Map)`, `ef (Visual effects)`, and `ft (Fighters)`, which makes future object additions easier to place.
Did not pretend the port solved splits or symbols; the PR documented that this was a preparatory configuration migration with limited immediate ninja-build effect.
Left anomalous/orphaned data entries visible instead of silently deleting them, while documenting that they were likely caused by erroneous splits.
decomp_lessons: When migrating decomp build metadata, preserve object order, library grouping, and matching/nonmatching state because these are part of the projectâ€™s reconstruction knowledge.
A configuration-only port can be valuable even before it affects the final build, because it centralizes future split/symbol work in the new build system.
Do not over-clean object lists during a mechanical port; questionable orphaned data files should be tracked and fixed separately when the split cause is understood.
Allow duplicate source paths with different matching statuses when the current split state requires it; forcing a one-source-one-status model may lose important build information.
Large object-list ports should use searchable subsystem labels and consistent object constructors so future decomp PRs can make targeted edits.
naming_conventions: Library labels use a short subsystem prefix plus a human-readable description, e.g. `lb (Library)`, `cm (Camera)`, `pl (Player)`, `mp (Map)`, `ef (Visual effects)`, `ft (Fighters)`.
Object status names are capitalized aliases: `Matching = True` and `NonMatching = False`.
Object entries use repository-relative C source paths like `melee/lb/lbcommand.c` and `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Wait.c`.
Unknown or address-derived files retain hexadecimal/address-style suffixes in names, such as `lb_00B0.c`, `lb_00CE.c`, `ef_061D.c`, `ft_07C1.c`, and `ftCo_08A6.c`.
Fighter common-state files follow the `ftCo_` prefix convention under `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/`.

Character-specific fighter files use abbreviated character prefixes in subdirectories, such as `ftMario/ftMr_*`, `ftCaptain/ftCa_*`, `ftFox/ftFx_*`, `ftLink/ftLk_*`, `ftDonkey/ftDk_*`, and `ftSeak/ftSk_*`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Encoded match state at the object-list level with `Object(Matching, ...)` and `Object(NonMatching, ...)` rather than relying on comments or separate lists.
Preserved duplicate matching/nonmatching entries for some files, which can model partial object splits, mixed data/text status, or unresolved split boundaries.
Kept orphaned data files in the configuration despite noting they should not exist, avoiding accidental loss of current split information during the port.
This PR did not add new assembly, splits, or symbols according to its body; its matching relevance is in build metadata organization.

review_feedback.feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the supplied context.
Author noted the port does not add splits or symbols and therefore does basically nothing to the `ninja` build process yet.
Author noted several orphaned data files, such as `baselib_shared_data_001`, should not exist and are products of erroneous splits to clean up later.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove or fix orphaned data files such as `baselib_shared_data_001`.
Find subsequent PRs where `config.libs` begins affecting the `ninja` build process.
Find later changes to `RuntimeLib` to see whether runtime libraries diverged from plain `Lib(...)` handling.
Audit duplicate `Object(Matching, ...)` and `Object(NonMatching, ...)` entries for the same source path to determine which represent intentional partial splits versus stale configuration.
Compare this port against the removed or superseded `obj_files.mk` to verify all object ordering and grouping were preserved.
